payload.title: Rewrite OpenClaw core imports to dependency injection

payload.body:
## Task

All source files in src/ were copied from openclaw src/plugins/lcm/ and src/agents/tools/lcm-*.ts. They still have imports pointing to OpenClaw internals.

Read specs/extraction-plan.md for the full import rewrite map.
Read src/types.ts for the LcmDependencies interface.

### Phase 1: Simple files — already clean
- src/db/config.ts ✅
- src/store/fts5-sanitize.ts ✅
- src/store/index.ts ✅

### Phase 2: Connection + migration
- src/db/connection.ts — replace requireNodeSqlite with direct better-sqlite3 import
- src/db/migration.ts — fix internal imports

### Phase 3: Core engine files — thread LcmDependencies
Each file that imports from OpenClaw core needs to accept LcmDependencies as a parameter and use injected deps instead of direct imports. Key files: summarize.ts, assembler.ts, compaction.ts, engine.ts, expansion*.ts, integrity.ts, large-files.ts, retrieval.ts

### Phase 4: Tool files
- Create src/tools/common.ts with local jsonResult, readStringParam, AnyAgentTool
- Refactor each tool to factory function: createLcmGrepTool(deps) => AnyAgentTool
- Replace context-engine imports with openclaw/plugin-sdk

### Phase 5: Wire up index.ts
- Construct LcmDependencies from OpenClawPluginApi
- Register context engine and all tools

### Phase 6: Fix test imports
- Update test import paths, mock LcmDependencies

### Constraints
- Keep @mariozechner/pi-agent-core as peer dep for message types
- ContextEngine interface from openclaw/plugin-sdk
- Pure import/DI refactor — no business logic changes
- Verify with npx tsc --noEmit

payload.description: Implement the full spec at specs/summary-presentation-and-depth-aware-prompts.md. Five phases: (1) schema migration for earliest_at, latest_at, descendant_count columns with backfill, (2) XML summary presentation format in assembler.ts replacing informal headers, (3) depth-dispatched summarization prompts (leaf, d1, d2, d3+) replacing single Pi-style template, (4) timestamp injection in compaction leafPass and condensedPass, (5) SummaryStore type/read/write updates.
payload.title: Depth-aware prompts, XML presentation, and schema additions
Fix all 24 broken vitest tests

payload.body:
All 24 test files fail on module resolution. Two categories:

1. **Relative path mismatch**: Tests in test/ import from ./foo.js (e.g. ./assembler.js, ./engine.js, ./db/config.js) as if co-located with src/. Need vitest.config.ts with path aliases or rewrite imports to ../src/foo.js.

2. **Stale monorepo imports**: Some tests still reference OpenClaw core internals:
   - summarize.test.ts imports from ../../agents/model-auth.js, ../../agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.js, ../../providers/github-copilot-token.js
   - lcm-tools.test.ts imports from ../../plugins/lcm/expansion-auth.js, ../../context-engine/init.js, ../../context-engine/registry.js
   These need to be rewritten to use the plugin's own exports or properly mocked.

**Fix plan:**
1. Create vitest.config.ts with resolve.alias mapping or just fix all import paths
2. Fix category 1: rewrite ./foo.js to ../src/foo.js in all test files
3. Fix category 2: rewrite monorepo imports to use local plugin paths + proper mocks
4. Ensure tsconfig includes test files (currently excludes **/*.test.ts)
5. Run npx vitest run — all 24 suites should pass
6. Do NOT change any business logic — import/config fixes only

payload.description:
Review all uses of the name 'openclaw-lcm', 'open-lcm', '@martian-engineering/openclaw-lcm' across the entire repo and produce a comprehensive rename specification for changing the project name to 'lossless-claw' (npm: '@martian-engineering/lossless-claw' or similar).

Audit should cover:
- package.json (name, description, repository, homepage)
- README.md (all references)
- docs/*.md (all references)
- Source code: any string literals, comments, log messages referencing the old name
- Go module path in tui/go.mod
- .goreleaser.yml (binary names, project name)
- tui/Makefile
- GitHub repo name implications (remote URLs, links in docs)
- Import paths if package name changes
- Any references in test files

Output: a checklist of every file and line that needs to change, with proposed new values. Do NOT make the changes — just document them.
payload.title: Audit and spec rename from openclaw-lcm to lossless-claw

payload.description: In the sessions list screen, display an estimate of the total tokens contained in each session JSONL file. Use the existing len/4 estimator. Helps users gauge raw content size before drilling in.
payload.title: TUI sessions view: show estimated total tokens per session file

payload.description: OpenClaw security scanner flags engine.ts as env-harvesting (false positive from process.env + 'post' in comment). Move all process.env reads out of engine.ts into LcmConfig/snapshotPluginEnv in index.ts. See specs/env-config-extraction.md for full plan.
payload.title: Extract env var reads from engine.ts into config module

payload.description: Implement specs/historical-session-backfill.md: new backfill command for importing pre-LCM session JSONL, iterative depth-aware compaction, optional single-root fold, optional transplant-to target conversation, tests + docs.
payload.title: Add lcm-tui historical session backfill command

payload.description:
Implement the investigation/spec in specs/context-assembly-compaction-perf-overhead-spec.md. Scope includes low-overhead instrumentation, config flags, and benchmark methodology to quantify baseline vs LCM overhead on realistic turns.

Objectives:
1. Measure context assembly overhead per turn.
2. Measure token estimation/serialization overhead per turn.
3. Measure compaction decision/execution overhead per turn.
4. Measure instrumentation tax (disabled vs sampled/full).

Deliverables:
1. Configurable perf instrumentation in LCM code paths.
2. Structured perf outputs suitable for aggregation.
3. Reproducible benchmark runbook and results summary.
4. Docs updates for flags and interpretation.
payload.title: Measure per-turn LCM context assembly/compaction overhead

payload.description:
Implement foundational perf tracing with near-zero disabled-path overhead.

Reference spec: specs/context-assembly-compaction-perf-overhead-spec.md

Acceptance criteria:
1. Add LCM perf env/config fields with sane defaults:
   - LCM_PERF_ENABLED
   - LCM_PERF_SAMPLE_RATE
   - LCM_PERF_SLOW_TURN_MS
   - LCM_PERF_LOG_LEVEL
   - LCM_PERF_OUTPUT
   - LCM_PERF_OUTPUT_PATH
   - LCM_PERF_INCLUDE_DB_COUNTS
   - LCM_PERF_INCLUDE_SERIALIZE_STATS
   - LCM_PERF_REDACT_SESSION_ID
2. Add a no-op tracer implementation and enabled tracer implementation with monotonic timers.
3. Disabled mode incurs no meaningful allocations/work at call sites (guard-first API).
4. Route perf emissions through LCM logger surface (not raw console logging for new perf events).
5. Unit tests cover config parsing and tracer behavior.

File touch map:
- src/db/config.ts
- src/types.ts
- index.ts
- src/perf/perf-trace.ts (new)
- test/* (config/tracer coverage)
- docs/configuration.md
- README.md
payload.title: Add low-overhead perf instrumentation scaffolding and config flags

payload.description:
Add per-turn and step-level instrumentation to assembly and token estimation paths.

Reference spec: specs/context-assembly-compaction-perf-overhead-spec.md

Acceptance criteria:
1. Emit per-turn summary metrics for assemble path (duration, counts, tokens).
2. Emit sampled step metrics for:
   - assemble.precheck
   - assemble.fetch_context_items
   - assemble.resolve_items
   - assemble.resolve_message_item
   - assemble.resolve_summary_item
3. Capture token-estimation/serialization counters where enabled.
4. Preserve existing behavior and return values of engine/assembler methods.
5. Add tests validating metrics presence and sampling behavior.

File touch map:
- src/engine.ts
- src/assembler.ts
- src/store/conversation-store.ts (only if lightweight counters need hook points)
- src/store/summary-store.ts (only if lightweight counters need hook points)
- test/engine.test.ts
- test/*assembler* (add/update)
payload.title: Instrument assembly and token-estimation hotspots

payload.description:
Add step-level instrumentation for compaction loops and summarization normalization/retry paths.

Reference spec: specs/context-assembly-compaction-perf-overhead-spec.md

Acceptance criteria:
1. Emit compaction metrics for evaluate/leaf/sweep/condensed phases.
2. Emit summarization call metrics separating:
   - provider completion latency
   - normalization/extraction latency
   - retry/fallback path activation
3. Include token before/after counters and compaction trigger metadata.
4. Keep perf output sampled/thresholded according to config.
5. Add tests validating emitted fields on normal and fallback summarization flows.

File touch map:
- src/engine.ts
- src/compaction.ts
- src/summarize.ts
- test/engine.test.ts
- test/summarize.test.ts
- test/*compaction* (add/update)
payload.title: Instrument compaction and summarization hotspots

payload.description:
Create benchmark workflow and produce overhead comparison artifacts.

Reference spec: specs/context-assembly-compaction-perf-overhead-spec.md

Acceptance criteria:
1. Define reproducible workload scenarios (short chat, coding/tool-heavy, long-running, file-heavy).
2. Run matrix:
   - A0 baseline legacy context engine
   - A1 LCM perf disabled
   - A2 LCM perf enabled sampled
   - A3 LCM perf enabled full sample (controlled)
3. Report p50/p90/p99 for turn, assembly, compaction, and instrumentation overhead deltas.
4. Publish runbook + interpretation guide in docs.
5. Provide recommendation thresholds for default sampling and slow-turn logging.

File touch map:
- scripts/bench/turn-overhead.ts (new)
- docs/perf/context-overhead-benchmark.md (new)
- docs/configuration.md
- README.md
payload.title: Run baseline-vs-LCM overhead experiments and publish runbook

payload.description: Start with only fix #1 from perf plan: replace full conversation load with keyset/cursor-based windowed loading in TUI. Requirements: (1) initial open loads newest window only (configurable default window size), (2) reload preserves window behavior (no full history hydration), (3) explicit load-older/load-newer pagination commands or keybind path, (4) keyset pagination on stable cursor (message id), not OFFSET, (5) maintain current UX correctness for selection/focus after page transitions, (6) add instrumentation/timing logs around query duration and render latency, (7) add tests for paging boundaries and no-regression of ordering. Keep scope strictly to windowed loading; defer virtualization/lazy-body unless required by architecture.
payload.title: Implement windowed message loading in TUI conversation view

payload.description:
Instead of requiring users to manually add LCM usage instructions to AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md, generate them dynamically in the assemble() method via the new systemPromptAddition field on AssembleResult.

Instructions should include:
- LCM recall priority (lcm_grep -> lcm_describe -> lcm_expand_query)
- Expand-before-asserting guidance
- Uncertainty checklist
- Refusal-to-guess rule for exact claims from summaries
- Context-aware: skip expand guidance when no summaries exist, emphasize it when heavily compacted
- Version-coupled: instructions update with plugin code, no workspace file drift

Depends on clawdbot-e98 (AssembleResult.systemPromptAddition field).
payload.title: Generate dynamic system prompt instructions from assemble()

payload.body: Depends on clawdbot-e98 (AssembleResult.systemPromptAddition field in OpenClaw context-engine)

payload.description: openclaw.plugin.json declares incrementalMaxDepth with minimum: 0, but the runtime and README both support -1 to mean unlimited incremental condensation. OpenClaw rejects a config using -1 and the gateway fails to start until the manifest is fixed.
payload.title: Manifest blocks incrementalMaxDepth=-1

payload.description:
Reproduced on March 8, 2026 in /Users/josh/Projects/lossless-claw against installed openclaw 2026.2.17 and published plugin tarballs 0.2.1 and 0.2.2.

Using OpenClaw's real createPluginRegistry()/createApi() implementation from node_modules/openclaw/dist/plugin-sdk/registry-CvUzfgyU.js, the plugin API object does not include registerContextEngine. Calling plugin.register(api) throws:

TypeError: api.registerContextEngine is not a function

This occurs for both published versions 0.2.1 and 0.2.2. It means the current plugin cannot register its context engine on stock OpenClaw 2026.2.17.

Notes:
- 0.2.1 README still documented a custom OpenClaw build requirement.
- 0.2.2 removed that note, but the compatibility issue is still present locally.
- This is separate from plugin config parsing.
payload.title: Lossless Claw fails on stock OpenClaw because plugin API lacks registerContextEngine

payload.description:
Reproduced on March 8, 2026 against published tarballs 0.2.1 and 0.2.2.

In OpenClaw's real plugin load order, plugin config is validated against openclaw.plugin.json and passed as api.pluginConfig, but configSchema.parse() is not invoked before register().

Findings:
- 0.2.1 ignores plugin config entirely and always resolves defaults/env vars.
- 0.2.2 added _parsedPluginConfig populated from configSchema.parse(), but OpenClaw runtime does not call parse(), so register() still sees undefined and falls back to defaults.
- Separately, manifest/resolver key names do not match for at least two fields:
  - manifest uses dbPath, resolver reads pluginConfig.databasePath
  - manifest uses largeFileThresholdTokens, resolver reads pluginConfig.largeFileTokenThreshold

Result: plugins.entries.lossless-claw.config is silently ignored in stock load order, and even explicit support would still miss dbPath/large-file threshold until key names are aligned.
payload.title: Lossless Claw plugin config is ignored by runtime; manifest keys also mismatch resolver keys

payload.description:
Observed on March 8, 2026 while running `npx vitest` in /Users/josh/Projects/lossless-claw on branch lossless-claw-0e3/fix-plugin-config.

Result:
- 16 test files ran
- 14 passed
- 2 failed (`test/engine.test.ts`, `test/migration.test.ts`)
- 39 tests failed with the same root cause: `Error: no such module: fts5`

Representative stack:
- runLcmMigrations src/db/migration.ts:519
- CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE messages_fts USING fts5(...)

This appears to be an environment/runtime capability issue in the local Node `node:sqlite` build rather than a regression from the plugin-config fix. Targeted config tests still pass.
payload.title: Full Vitest suite fails locally when node:sqlite lacks FTS5 support

payload.description:
Review follow-up from PR #11.

The new normalizeAssistantReasoningBlocks() logic in src/transcript-repair.ts rewrites any assistant message that contains only reasoning/thinking and tool-call blocks by moving all reasoning blocks before all tool calls whenever it sees reasoning after a tool call.

Repro shape:
- assistant content: [function_call(fc_1), reasoning("reason for second"), function_call(fc_2)]
- repaired content becomes: [reasoning("reason for second"), function_call(fc_1), function_call(fc_2)]

That is more than transcript repair; it changes the relative semantics of interleaved multi-call content. If OpenAI or another provider ever emits multiple function calls with intervening reasoning, the current repair step can misattribute the reasoning and alter conversation meaning.

Suggested direction: constrain the repair to the specific OpenClaw-normalized single-call case, or preserve local adjacency rather than globally grouping reasoning blocks.
payload.title: Preserve per-call ordering when repairing OpenAI reasoning blocks

payload.description:
Observed on March 8, 2026 after deploying lossless-claw 0.2.4.

Gateway logs show:
- createLcmSummarize: resolveModel FAILED: No model configured for LCM summarization.
- resolveSummarize falls back to EMERGENCY TRUNCATION.

Current plugin code in index.ts snapshots LCM_SUMMARY_MODEL, LCM_SUMMARY_PROVIDER, and OPENCLAW_PROVIDER, but it does not capture or read OpenClaw's configured primary model. The resolver therefore throws unless LCM_SUMMARY_MODEL is explicitly set, even when OpenClaw already has agents.defaults.model.primary configured.

Expected behavior:
- If no explicit LCM summary model is configured, use OpenClaw's active/default agent model.
- Only require explicit LCM_* overrides when the operator wants a different summarization model.

Impact:
- Compaction degrades to emergency truncation instead of real summarization.
- This is a correctness/operability bug, not just missing docs.
payload.title: LCM summarizer does not inherit OpenClaw's default model

payload.description:
The top-level README is comprehensive but hard to scan for first-time users.

Observed issues:
- The optional FTS5/macOS build workflow appears inside the main Installation path and dominates the setup section.
- The README duplicates substantial reference material that already exists in docs/architecture.md, docs/agent-tools.md, docs/tui.md, and tui/README.md.
- There is no short quick-start / verify-success path for a normal install.
- The document mixes end-user install steps, operator troubleshooting, agent tool reference, TUI reference, and contributor guidance in one linear flow.

Desired outcome:
- Keep the README as a concise entry point with a strong quick start, a short feature overview, a minimal config section, a verify-success section, and links out to dedicated reference docs.
- Move advanced FTS5 guidance behind a troubleshooting or advanced setup section/link.
- Add a table of contents or equivalent navigation aid if the README remains long.
payload.title: Restructure README for progressive onboarding

payload.description: Conversation open in lcm-tui can spend 10s+ in render when a window contains very large tool outputs. Fix the conversation render path so oversized per-message display payloads are capped before wrap/styling, without changing stored message data.
payload.title: Avoid multi-second TUI stalls on large tool messages

payload.description: Implement the repo-side release workflow for lossless-claw based on /tmp/lossless-claw-release-workflow-investigation.md. Goal: replace manual version/changelog/tag handling with a reviewable release PR plus a manually triggered GitHub Actions publish flow using npm trusted publishing. Do not backfill tags in this effort.
payload.title: Build npm release workflow with changesets and trusted publishing

payload.body: Duplicate of lossless-claw-566 created during SQLite lock contention while setting up the release workflow epic.
Duplicate of lossless-claw-2dc.1 created during initial pebbles setup contention.
Duplicate of lossless-claw-2dc.3 created during initial pebbles setup contention.

Repo-side release workflow scaffolding is implemented and validated locally. Remaining work requires maintainer action outside the repo: configure npm trusted publishing for .github/workflows/publish.yml, optionally add a protected GitHub Environment, and then exercise the first release through the manual publish workflow.
Maintainer completed the external setup: npm trusted publisher configured, GitHub environment npm-publish created, required reviewer set to Josh, self-review remains allowed.

payload.description: PR #50 adds expansionModel/expansionProvider config keys and docs. The runtime change in src/tools/lcm-expand-query-tool.ts only threads expansionModel into the gateway agent call. expansionProvider is parsed in src/db/config.ts and documented in openclaw.plugin.json/README.md, but it is never consumed, so setting it has no effect. Review context: this makes the new provider override a no-op while advertising support for it.
payload.title: PR 50: expansionProvider config is parsed but not applied to delegated expansion sub-agent

payload.description: Review follow-up for PR #28: add tests covering tokenizer cold-start initialization, lockfile/dependency consistency, and proxy/logging safety; then fix implementation so precise counting works on first use without broad proxy side effects.
payload.title: Fix PR 28 tokenizer cold-start and packaging regressions
